content.problemStatement:
You are given a sequence A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã. Find the number of ways to split it into one or more non-empty contiguous subsequences B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BkB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_kB1‚Äã,B2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Bk‚Äã such that the following condition is satisfied: for each iii (1‚â§i‚â§k1 \le i \le k1‚â§i‚â§k), the XOR of all elements in BiB_iBi‚Äã is divisible by 2i‚àí12^{i-1}2i‚àí1.

Since this number can be enormous, compute it modulo 1,000,000,0071,000,000,0071,000,000,007 (109+710^9 + 7109+7).


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äî the number of ways to split AAA modulo 1,000,000,0071,000,000,0071,000,000,007 (109+710^9 + 7109+7).
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 100100100
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 1,0001,0001,000
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2,5002,5002,500
